manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: card, pass, spine, bug, school, bottle, mole, root, berry.
Your opponent's team words are: cell, police, germany, beach, spring, fighter, mail, jupiter.
Distractor words are: hospital, fall, embassy, fire, czech, box, wave.
The kill words are: belt.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: card, pass, spine, bug, school, bottle, mole, root, berry.
Your opponent's team words are: cell, police, germany, beach, spring, fighter, mail, jupiter.
Distractor words are: hospital, fall, embassy, fire, czech, box, wave.
The kill words are: belt.

CLUE: YARD
TARGETS: school, root, berry, mole, bug

The word school was guessed correctly. The word police was guessed but is an opponent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: card, pass, spine, bug, bottle, mole, root, berry.
Remaining words for your opponent are: cell, germany, beach, spring, fighter, mail, jupiter.
Remaining distractor words are: hospital, fall, embassy, fire, czech, box, wave.
Remaining kill words are: belt.

Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Goal: Provide a single-word clue related to some of my team's remaining words.
    *   Format:
        CLUE: <WORD>
        TARGETS: <TARGETS>
    *   Constraints:
        *   Single-word clue.
        *   Comma-separated list of target words.
        *   No other text.
        *   Clue cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.
        *   Avoid opponent words, distractor words, and kill words.
    *   Context: This is a Codenames-like game.
    *   Current State:
        *   My Team Words (Remaining): card, pass, spine, bug, bottle, mole, root, berry.

        *   Note: "school" is removed from my list. "police" is removed from opponent list (though technically it's still out there as a revealed opponent card, but the prompt says "Remaining words for your opponent are..." and lists police as gone? Wait, the prompt says "Remaining words for your opponent are: cell, germany, beach, spring, fighter, mail, jupiter." So "police" is gone/revealed. Good.)

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: card, pass, spine, bug, school, bottle, mole, root, berry.
Your opponent's team words are: cell, police, germany, beach, spring, fighter, mail, jupiter.
Distractor words are: hospital, fall, embassy, fire, czech, box, wave.
The kill words are: belt.

CLUE: YARD
TARGETS: school, root, berry, mole, bug

Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.
